# Research - 2026-06-15

Focus: **all** (CLI, providers, tools, MCP, commands, hooks, skills, plugins, agents, permissions)

> 1-day delta on 2026-06-14. Open `enhancement` issues #775-#778 cover yesterday's
> #1-#4 (roots, cwd, head+tail, ranged read). Yesterday's #5 (plugin command
> `submitPrompt`) shipped overnight as commit `df75474d` (PR #781). cline pushed
> a benchmark-driven cluster of follow-ups today: #11504 (search cap), #11509
> (apply_patch hunk skip), #11510 (zero-result success), #11518 (heredoc
> coalesce), #11520 (media budget). opencode shipped #32244 (MCP isError),
> #32245 (idle OAuth listener), #32309 (`@`-mention scoring), #32360 (escape
> consolidation). Three of those map cleanly to alexi gaps; the others are
> N/A (no OAuth callbacks, no `@`-mention picker, single-string `bash` tool).

## Executive Summary

- **cline #11509 -> Item #1 (~3h)**: `apply-patch.ts:25-85` walks
  patch lines blindly. ` ` context lines copy `lines[lineIndex]`
  without checking they match the patch's expected text. Bad
  hunks silently no-op and the model thinks the edit landed.
- **cline #11504 -> Item #2 (~3h)**: `codesearch.ts` (491 LOC)
  never imports `truncateOutput`. One broad query over the
  monorepo dumps every match unbounded into the model context.
- **opencode #32244 -> Item #3 (~2h)**: `client.ts:447-457`
  filters MCP error content to `type === 'text'` only, dropping
  `image`/`resource` error parts. Also flattens with `\n` instead
  of preserving order metadata when text parts coexist.
- **cline #11510 -> N/A**: our `grep.ts:681-693` already returns
  `success: true` on zero matches. Verified.
- **cline #11518 -> N/A**: our `bash` tool takes
  `command: z.string()` (line 14), not `commands: string[]`.
  Heredoc-split is structurally impossible.
- **opencode #32309/#32360 -> N/A**: no TUI `@`-mention picker
  and no provider OAuth callbacks in our codebase.
- **cline #11520 -> Skip**: 2122-LOC media-budget refactor.
  `transform.ts` is 58 lines and has zero image/base64 paths.
- **Carryover from 06-14**: items #1-#4 are filed as #775
  (roots), #776 (cwd), #778 (head+tail), #777 (ranged read).
  None labelled `auto-implement` -> queue is still 0. See
  Recommendation 1 + already-open #773.

## Detailed Findings

#### 1. `apply_patch` must validate context and fail on skipped hunks (Category: tool, ~3h)

- **What**: In `applyPatchToContent` (`src/tool/tools/apply-patch.ts:25-85`),
  validate ` ` context and `-` deletion lines against `lines[lineIndex]`
  before copying/skipping. On mismatch, throw a typed `PatchHunkError`
  carrying the hunk number, file path, expected vs actual line, and
  surrounding context. In `execute` (line 130-208), catch that error
  before `fs.writeFile` so partial application never reaches disk.
  Fuzzy matches that DO apply still succeed; only hard mismatches abort.
- **Why**: Today the loop blindly copies `lines[lineIndex]` for context
  and increments `lineIndex` for deletions without checking the patch
  matches reality. If the model emits a stale hunk against a moved
  function, we write bogus output and report `success: true`. The
  model proceeds from a false premise. cline measured this as a
  benchmark regression source on Harbor agent traces.
- **Source**: cline/cline#11509 (commit `d8eb0631`, 2026-06-12, `+50 -5`).
- **Category**: tool. **Size**: small (~3h).

#### 2. Cap `codebase_search` output via `truncateOutput` (Category: tool, ~3h)

- **What**: In `src/tool/tools/codesearch.ts`, import
  `truncateOutput` from `../index.js` and apply it to the formatted
  match list before returning. Add a per-query result cap (suggest
  100 matches, matching cline) and a 48 KB string cap matching our
  `MAX_BYTES`. When truncation triggers, set `truncated: true` and
  populate `hint` with a "narrow your pattern with `include` or a
  more specific query" message. Tool description must document the
  cap so the model plans accordingly.
- **Why**: `codesearch.ts` is 491 LOC and never calls `truncateOutput`
  - the only file under `src/tool/tools/` that returns formatted
  text without it (verified via `grep -n truncateOutput src/tool/tools/*.ts`).
  One broad query like `function` over a 30k-file monorepo dumps
  unbounded matches with context lines into the model turn, blowing
  the context window in one call. `grep.ts:679` already caps at 1000
  matches; this aligns the two surfaces.
- **Source**: cline/cline#11504 (commit `e91cba40`, 2026-06-15, `+159 -37`).
- **Category**: tool. **Size**: small (~3h).

#### 3. Preserve all MCP error content parts (Category: mcp, ~2h)

- **What**: In `src/mcp/client.ts:447-457`, when `result.isError` is
  true, do not filter `content` to `type === 'text'`. Serialize
  non-text parts via `JSON.stringify({ type, ...rest })` joined with
  newlines, OR format `image` parts as
  `[image: <mimeType>, <bytes> bytes omitted]` and `resource` parts
  as `[resource: <uri>]`. Keep the existing `structuredContent`
  fast path (line 437-444) unchanged.
- **Why**: Spec-compliant MCP servers (filesystem, ast-grep, several
  Codex-pattern servers) can return `isError: true` with mixed
  content arrays - e.g. an error string PLUS a `resource` link to
  the offending file. Our current code drops every non-text part
  silently, so the model sees `Unknown error` (line 457 fallback)
  for what was actually a structured failure. opencode landed this
  as part of the `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk@1.29.0` patch series.
- **Source**: sst/opencode#32244 (commit `dfb616f0`, 2026-06-15, `+33 -0`).
- **Category**: mcp. **Size**: small (~2h).

## Actionable Recommendations

1. **Apply `auto-implement` label to #775-#778 and any new issues**.
   Issue #773 (label-on-create) is already filed but not yet shipped.
   Until then, daily-merge-prs sees queue=0 and engineering idles.
   Manual workaround: `gh issue edit 775 776 777 778 --add-label auto-implement`.
2. **Smallest-PR ladder for today's items**: #3 (~2h, ~30 LOC,
   `src/mcp/client.ts` only) -> #1 (~3h, ~80 LOC, `apply-patch.ts`
   + new test) -> #2 (~3h, ~50 LOC, `codesearch.ts` + import).
   #1 and #2 are independent; they can ship in parallel PRs. #3
   composes naturally with the still-open #775 (roots) - bundle.
3. **Reference-repo signal-to-noise**: aider had 0 actionable in
   24 days, claude-code is CHANGELOG-only for 14 days. #772 (filed)
   demotes both to weekly. Today opencode + cline carried the
   entire signal: 5/5 actionable items came from those two repos.
